title: VimFu — :{range}!{filter} — Filter Lines
description: Pipe lines through an external command with :{range}!{filter}. Use :%!sort to sort the whole file.

setup.comment: Set TERM for proper color support in Neovim

setup.content:
cherry
apple
banana
date
elderberry
fig
grape

steps.say: You can pipe buffer lines through an external command. The output replaces those lines.
Sort the entire file. Percent means all lines, bang sort runs the sort command.
The whole file is now sorted alphabetically. The lines were piped through sort and replaced.

steps.comment: --- Demo: undo and sort a range ---

steps.say: Undo that. Now sort just lines two through four.
Only those three lines were sorted. The rest of the file is untouched.
Range bang filter. Pipe any lines through any shell command. Sort, uniq, awk, anything.
